q: That restaurant (be) a clothes shop but not now.
a: That restaurant used to be a clothes shop but not now.

q: We (have) a computer, but we do now.
a: We did not used to have a computer, but we do now.

q: David Beckham (play) for Manchester United.
a: David Beckham used to play for Manchester United.

q: (they/work) together?
a: Did they used to work together?

q: I read more books in the past than I do now.
a: I used to read more books than I do now.

q: He didn't drink coffee before, but now he has a cup every morning.
a: He didn't used to drink coffee, but now he has a cup every morning.

q: I played soccer every weekend in the past.
a: I used to play soccer every weekend.

q: I didn't enjoy spicy food before, but now I love it.
a: I didn't used to enjoy spicy food, but now I love it.

q: She walked to school every day previously.
a: She used to walk to school every day.

q: They (not travel) abroad because they couldn't afford the tickets back then.
a: They didn't used to travel abroad because they couldn't afford the tickets back then.

q: Our neighbors (have) a big dog that always barked at strangers.
a: Our neighbors used to have a big dog that always barked at strangers.

q: We (not go) to the beach often because our town was far from the sea.
a: We didn't used to go to the beach often because our town was far from the sea.

q: My father (drive) a very old car before he bought a new one last year.
a: My father used to drive a very old car before he bought a new one last year.

q: (Where / she / work) before she got a job at the bank?
a: Where did she used to work before she got a job at the bank?

q: He (not like) vegetables, but now he eats them every day for his health.
a: He didn't used to like vegetables, but now he eats them every day for his health.

q: I (not speak) much English until I started taking online classes.
a: I didn't used to speak much English until I started taking online classes.

q: Our teacher (give) us lots of homework, but we didn't mind because we loved her.
a: Our teacher used to give us lots of homework, but we didn't mind because we loved her.

q: (What / your brother / do) before he moved to Singapore for work?
a: What did your brother used to do before he moved to Singapore for work?

q: My grandparents (live) in the countryside, but now they live in the city.
a: My grandparents used to live in the countryside, but now they live in the city.

q: People (write) letters to keep in touch before smartphones became popular.
a: People used to write letters to keep in touch before smartphones became popular.

q: We (spend) the whole summer at our grandparents' farm when we were kids.
a: We used to spend the whole summer at our grandparents' farm when we were kids.

q: When I was a child, I (watch) cartoons every Saturday morning before breakfast.
a: When I was a child, I used to watch cartoons every Saturday morning before breakfast.

q: He (not wear) glasses before, but now he needs them to read.
a: He didn't used to wear glasses before, but now he needs them to read.

q: (Did / your school / organize) school trips every year when you were a student?
a: Did your school used to organize school trips every year when you were a student?

q: My mother (cook) dinner for the whole family every evening when I was in high school.
a: My mother used to cook dinner for the whole family every evening when I was in high school.

q: (Where / you / hang out) with your friends after school?
a: Where did you used to hang out with your friends after school?

q: I (not enjoy) reading novels until my friend recommended some good ones.
a: I didn't used to enjoy reading novels until my friend recommended some good ones.

q: (Did / your family / eat) together at the table when you were younger?
a: Did your family used to eat together at the table when you were younger?

q: (Did / you / play) the piano when you were in middle school?
a: Did you used to play the piano when you were in middle school?
